notes: Recording may be brittle — see assessment.json caveats. Newer Claude Code (2.1.x+) writes a NEW transcript file at the compaction boundary, which can register as a fresh session row to irrlicht depending on session-stitching state (covered separately by session-resume). irrlicht's CompactionState domain field is dead code (no production writer), so this spec asserts only the visible state arc: ready→working→ready around /compact, then a normal turn afterward to prove the session continues. The compaction-vs-normal-turn distinction is not observable today.

text: First user prompt populates context: ready → working
First turn completes back to ready

After /compact runs, the session settles back to ready (claude does the compaction in-memory / writes a synthesized boundary). State must not stick in waiting. The state may briefly enter working during /compact's summarizer call — only the post-compaction settle to ready is asserted here.
Post-compaction user prompt drives ready → working — proves the session continues normally after /compact
Post-compaction turn completes back to ready
